Civil Liberties.summary:
India recorded the highest number of internet shutdowns among democracies in 2025, according to a report by Access Now, which documented 65 such instances. 

A bill in India provides statutory recognition to platform-based gig workers and proposes the creation of a tripartite welfare board, welcomed by the Telangana Gig Workers Union. 

A Special Investigation Team detained the son of a self-proclaimed godman, Kharat, for questioning in connection with allegations of rape and other crimes.
Civil Liberties.top_article_title: India recorded 65 internet shutdowns in 2025, highest among democracies: Access Now report

Welfare.summary:
Madhya Pradesh has seen a return to dung-cake stoves, undoing the progress made by the Ujjwala scheme which provided smoke-free kitchens. 

Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta has dismissed rumors about the 'Pink Saheli card', stating that some political people are spreading misinformation about the welfare scheme. 

The Delhi Development Authority (DDA) sold over 700 flats in Delhi within two days under the Narela Housing Schemes.
Welfare.top_article_title: Madhya Pradesh: Ujjwala gave them smoke-free kitchens, but now, dung-cake stoves are back

Fintech.summary: UPI PIN is no longer sufficient for online payments, and users will now need to authenticate with a fingerprint or one-time password (OTP) starting today. This change aims to enhance security for digital transactions.
Fintech.top_article_title: Your UPI PIN is no longer enough: Why your next online payment will require a fingerprint or OTP starting today

Urban.summary:
India has launched the world's largest census to count over 1.4 billion people, collecting data on housing, economics, and caste. The census aims to reshape welfare policies in the country.

The Mysuru City Corporation in India has introduced a motor vehicle cess and increased property tax by 3%. 

Chief Secretary Sanjay Gupta denies any involvement in a Himachal housing project row, accusing two retired bureaucrats of defaming him.
Urban.top_article_title: India begins biggest census to count 1.4 billion people, caste data to reshape politics

Health.summary:
The Census 2027 in India will be conducted entirely in digital mode, a change from the originally scheduled format, due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

Banks in India are not addressing the debt caused by climate disasters such as heatwaves, pollution, and floods.

In Maharashtra, India, local priests known as 'pujaris' are being enlisted as allies in the fight against malaria in the Gadchiroli region.
Health.top_article_title: Census 2027 to be entirely in digital mode starting today

Environment.summary:
India's carbon dioxide emissions in 2025 grew at the slowest rate in two decades, increasing by 0.5% in the second half of the year. 

Odisha is seeking permission to divert forest land for Tata Steel's iron ore block. 

Justice B V Nagarathna has called for corporate accountability and environmental justice, stating that profit should not be allowed to stand alone.
Environment.top_article_title: Analysis: India’s CO2 emissions in 2025 grew at slowest rate in two decades

The institution's actions are seen as a move to exclude important perspectives from the curriculum.

Telecom.summary: A report by Nokia predicts India's 5G subscriber base will exceed 1 billion by 2031, with average monthly mobile data consumption per user increasing to over 31 GB in 2025. India's average monthly mobile data consumption has crossed the 31 GB mark, with 5G traffic growing by 70%, and the country now has the world's second-largest 5G subscriber base. The Department of Telecommunications is extending the SIM binding deadline for messaging platforms like WhatsApp and Telegram to December to help resolve technical issues.
Telecom.top_article_title: India’s 5G subscriber base to exceed 1-billion by 2031: Nokia report

articles.title: Why does India’s financial year start from 1st April and not 1st January? Explained
articles.summary: India's financial year runs from April to March, aligning with agricultural cycles and administrative needs. This structure, inherited from British traditions, remains practical for government budgeting and rural economic planning.

articles.summary: Shares of Garden Reach Shipbuilders   Engineers jumped over 16% after the company reported a record FY26 turnover of Rs 6,400 crore, up 26% year-on-year. articles.title: Co-Owner Cannot Be Denied Enhanced Compensation Granted To Other Co-Owners Under Same Acquisition: Himachal Pradesh High Court
articles.summary: The Himachal Pradesh High Court has held that a co-owner whose land has been acquired is entitled to the same compensation as another co-owner, even if the former did not independently seek a reference under the Land Acquisition Act. The Court observed that once fair compensation is judicially determined for land acquired under the same notification, denying the same to similarly placed co-owners on technical grounds would amount to discrimination. A division bench comprising Justice Gurmeet Sin

articles.title: Talaq Takes Effect From Date Of Pronouncement, Subsequent Court Decree Merely Declaratory In Nature: Allahabad High Court
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court has observed that under Mohammedan Law, a divorce takes effect from the date a husband pronounces talaq and a subsequent court decree confirming it is merely declaratory in nature. A bench of Justice Madan Pal Singh clarified that such a court decree does not create a fresh divorce from the date of the judgment, but rather, it relates back to the original date of the talaq pronouncement. articles.summary: The Delhi High Court recently pulled up various media platforms including a journalist and actress Richa Chadha for their social media posts labelling a man a “molester” over allegations of sexual harassment arising out of an in-flight incident. Justice Vikas Mahajan said that the narratives set by the media houses and digital platforms clearly breached the contours of the FIR, and that the publications did not merely report the allegations in the FIR but they prematurely adjudicate the matter.

articles.title: Earning Mother Need Not Be Impleaded In Child Maintenance Claim Against Father But Court Must Assess Joint Income: Allahabad HC
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court has observed that an earning mother need not be formally impleaded as a party in a maintenance plea filed by a child against her/his father. A bench of Justice Madan Pal Singh , however, directed that in such cases, the trial court must consider the financial capacity of both earning parents while determining the final maintenance amount based on the principle of shared parental responsibility. The single judge passed this order while hearing a criminal revision petition

articles.title: How Toolsvilla Is Simplifying Agricultural Equipment Buying for Farmers
articles.summary: Did you know there’s a dedicated platform in India that’s changing how people buy equipment? For a long time, farmers and small business owners didn’t really have many options. Buying machinery usually meant going to the nearest dealer and choosing from whatever was available. There wasn’t much information to compare, and most decisions were made quickly often without knowing if it was the right fit.

articles.title: Dedicated rare earth corridors will reduce import dependence: Govt
articles.summary: India is establishing dedicated rare earth corridors in Odisha, Kerala, Andhra Pradesh, and Tamil Nadu to reduce import dependence and boost domestic capabilities in strategic sectors. These corridors will focus on mining, processing, research, and manufacturing, aligning with national goals of self-reliance and net-zero emissions. A joint working group is formulating guidelines to implement these initiatives.

A joint working group is formulating guidelines to implement these initiatives.

articles.title: NHAI raises Rs 28,307 crore through monetisation in 2025-26; hopeful of achieving the Rs 30,000 crore target for FY’26
articles.summary: The ministry of road transport and highways, on Monday, said the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) monetised over 310 km of National Highways under InvIT Round-5 for Rs 6366.98 crore while realising Rs 3,087 crore from the toll-operate-transfer (TOT) Bundle-18. With bids received for TOT Bundle-19, which are under technical evaluation, NHAI is well poised to achieve the government’s budgeted target of Rs 30,000 crore for current FY 2025–26, it said.
articles.raw_summary: The ministry of road transport and highways, on Monday, said the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) monetised over 310 km of National Highways under InvIT Round-5 for Rs 6366.98 crore while realising Rs 3,087 crore from the toll-operate-transfer (TOT) Bundle-18. With bids received for TOT Bundle-19, which are under technical evaluation, NHAI is well poised to achieve the government’s budgeted target of Rs 30,000 crore for current FY 2025–26, it said.

articles.title: Supervisory Data Quality Index for Scheduled Commercial Banks  (December 2025)
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has released Supervisory Data Quality Index (sDQI) for SCBs for December 2025 . It measures data quality in terms of the Accuracy, Timeliness, Completeness and Consistency in the submission of returns. The objective of sDQI is to assess the adherence to the principles enunciated in the Master Direction on Filing of Supervisory Returns 2024 . (Brij Raj)             Chief General Manager Press Release: 2025-2026/2361

articles.title: The Inimitable C.K. Daphtary

articles.title: CHRIST (Deemed to be University), Pune Hosts THEMIS 6th Moot Court Competition 2026
articles.summary: The School of Law, CHRIST (Deemed to be University), Pune Lavasa Campus , successfully hosted its flagship event, THEMIS – 6th Moot Court Competition, 2026, from March 27 to 29, 2026. The competition featured a prize pool of ₹3,00,000 , along with individual awards for Best Speaker, Best Researcher, and Best Memorial. The 2026 moot proposition focused on contemporary issues in constitutional law, federalism, and data governance, encouraging participants to engage with evolving legal challenges.

articles.title: Call For papers; Delhi Journal For Law And Justice Invites Submissions For 2026 Issue
articles.summary: The Delhi Journal for Law and Justice (DJLJ) is the student-run journal of Law Centre-II, Faculty of Law, University of Delhi . It is a double-blind peer-reviewed academic journal focused on publishing original, high-quality research in the field of law and justice. The journal encourages discussion on important legal issues and promotes critical engagement with legal theories and practices. It contributes to the ongoing discussion on justice, governance, rights, and duties in India and beyond.

articles.title: NLIU Bhopal Team Wins 5th National Moot Court Competition, 2026
articles.summary: The National Law Institute University (NLIU), Bhopal has announced that its team comprising Anaya Muchhal and Kriti Sharma as speakers, along with Vidhushi Jaiswal as researcher, emerged as the winner of the 5th National Moot Court Competition, 2026, organised by Army Law College, Pune. The competition was held on March 27–28, 2026 in Pune and focused on themes of Constitutional Law and the Information Technology Act, while also addressing contemporary legal issues relating to the Digital Person

articles.title: Writ Petition Not Maintainable Before Final Order In Court Martial Process: Delhi High Court
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court has held that a writ petition challenging disciplinary and court martial proceedings is not maintainable before the passing of a final order. A Division Bench of Justices V. Kameswar Rao and Manmeet Pritam Singh Arora observed, “...it is a settled position of law that till such time a final order is passed in the proceedings of this nature, any interference without waiting for the final decision on the proceedings by the High Court in a writ petition is uncalled for.” Relian

articles.summary: The Delhi High Court has held that a driving licence cannot be treated as fake merely because it was in a booklet form and had not been converted into a smart card. Justice Neena Bansal Krishna thus dismissed an appeal filed by an insurance company against a decree in favour of the insured. articles.title: Telangana relied on RBI borrowings for most of FY25: CAG report

This reliance on short-term borrowing mechanisms highlights financial management challenges. The CAG report detailed these transactions.

articles.title: Former Deloitte India chairman PR Ramesh joins VerSe Innovation as independent director
articles.summary: PR Ramesh, former Deloitte India chairman, has joined VerSe Innovation as an independent director and will chair its audit committee. He will oversee financial reporting, internal controls, risk management, and compliance, strengthening governance after prior audit concerns. VerSe, backed by CPPIB, Lupa Systems, Z47 and others, has raised over $2 billion and is valued at nearly $5 billion.

articles.title: Govt extends RoDTEP benefits for exporters till September 30
articles.summary: Indian exporters receive a six-month extension for RoDTEP scheme benefits until September 30. This move aims to counter trade disruptions from the West Asia crisis. The scheme offers refunds on duties and taxes for manufactured goods. This support helps exporters manage rising freight costs and trade risks.

articles.title: India grants temporary customs duty relief for SEZ goods sold domestically
articles.summary: India will offer temporary customs duty relief for goods produced in special economic zones and sold domestically. The reduced duty rates, ranging from 5% to 12.5%, will be effective from April 1, 2026, to March 31, 2027, benefiting businesses that commenced production by March 31, 2025.

articles.title: Courier export cap removed to boost ecomm shipments, cut logistics delays
articles.summary: Starting April 1, India has abolished the ₹10 lakh threshold on courier exports, a shift aimed at propelling e-commerce forward and supporting small enterprises. The introduction of a quick return mechanism for unclaimed parcels is also noteworthy, designed to alleviate shipping delays and enhance overall delivery efficiency for cross-border commerce.

articles.title: RBI's net short position rises to $77 billion in Feb as it defends rupee
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's net short position in forwards rose to $77 billion in February. This move impacts the central bank's capacity to protect the rupee. The forward book is projected to exceed $100 billion. This situation affects foreign exchange reserves and rupee liquidity. The market now closely watches import cover levels.

articles.title: US tariffs drag India apparel export growth to modest 1.5%: ICRA
articles.summary: Indian apparel exports saw modest growth, impacted by US tariffs. A weaker rupee helped boost earnings in local currency. Shipments to the UK and UAE provided some relief. ICRA forecasts stronger revenue growth and improved financial health for exporters in the coming year. Geopolitical risks remain a concern for the sector.

articles.title: Services, remittances boost India's invisible inflows to $464 billion in FY26
articles.summary: Receipts from the services sector, which accounted for two-third of the total, climbed about 9% to $310 billion, data showed. These are known as invisible receipts as these reflect earnings a country generates from IT services, tourism and remittances   transfers rather than international trade in physical goods.

articles.title: 10 foreign ships with energy cargo for India stranded in Persian Gulf
articles.summary: Ten foreign-flagged ships carrying energy cargo are stranded in the Persian Gulf, with 18 Indian vessels also in the region, raising concerns over Strait of Hormuz transit. The government prioritizes Indian ships, with two LPG carriers having successfully passed. Insurance costs have significantly increased due to the heightened risk.

articles.title: 'India's economy will double every 7-8 years,' says Union Minister Gajendra Singh Shekhawat
articles.summary: India's economy is set for significant growth, aiming for 30 trillion dollars by 2047. However, ongoing global conflicts pose risks. These tensions could reduce India's economic expansion by one percent and increase inflation by 1.5 percent. The nation's reliance on imported energy makes it vulnerable to these disruptions. Despite these challenges, India's economy shows strong momentum.

articles.title: Iran war shock: Middle East conflict could cut 1 ppt from India’s FY27 GDP outlook
articles.summary: Iran War: A persistent Middle East conflict could reduce India's real GDP growth by 1 percentage point and increase retail inflation by 1.5 percentage points from baseline estimates. This impact stems from disruptions to global oil and energy markets, affecting employment-intensive sectors and aggregate demand. The government may need to implement countercyclical policies and augment the Economic Stabilization Fund.

articles.title: "Unfortunate for India," Ex R&AW Chief hints at probable inflation if West Asia situation escalates

articles.title: India diesel exports jump 20% in March amid Iran war
articles.summary: India boosted diesel exports significantly in March. This surge was driven by favorable economics for middle distillates. Geopolitical events in West Asia tightened supply, increasing diesel and jet fuel prices. Refiners adjusted production to capitalize on these higher margins. India also increased domestic LPG production to compensate for lost imports. Overall refined product exports saw a slight decline.

articles.title: Pulses import duties likely to stay unchanged
articles.summary: New Delhi is set to maintain current import duties on pulses. The government will also extend the duty-free period for tur and urad. This move aims to prevent price increases. Officials anticipate an El Nino affecting monsoon rainfall. Production of tur and urad is expected to decline. This could lead to higher prices and food inflation.

articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India should use its substantial foreign exchange reserves to stabilize the Indian Rupee. A report from SBI suggests this action is necessary as the ongoing West Asia crisis impacts global markets. The report highlights that India possesses ample reserves to manage speculative moves.

articles.title: India's net international liability comes down by $10.9 billion in the third quarter

The ratio of India's international assets to liabilities saw a positive shift. Indian residents invested more abroad in direct investments and deposits.

The trend indicates a continued expansion in industrial undertakings.

articles.summary: Rising crude oil prices due to West Asian tensions could significantly push up India's inflation by 55-60 basis points per $10 barrel increase in FY27. articles.title: Government to likely halt disbursement under ECMS if design, Six Sigma quality not met: Ashwini Vaishnaw
articles.summary: The government is set to review its electronics manufacturing scheme. Beneficiaries must invest in product design and achieve Six Sigma standards. A shift towards logistics and supply chain activities is also evident.

articles.title: Strong domestic demand supports India's economy, but Middle East crisis raises stagflation risks: Morgan Stanley

External vulnerabilities remain a concern due to reliance on Middle Eastern energy and its importance for Indian exports and remittances.

articles.title: Mint Explainer | West Asia subsea cable threats: What could happen to India’s internet amid Iran war?

articles.title: What is the minimum amount to start trading in the Indian stock market?
articles.summary: You can start investing in the Indian stock market with as little as ₹100, but experts suggest ₹5,000–₹10,000 for diversification and cost efficiency. Platforms like HDFC Sky are making investing easier for beginners.
